theoremName: finite Schur Douglas repair algebra theorem
ambientSpace: finite-dimensional Hilbert space H=N direct-sum U
hypotheses: K is Hermitian with blocks A=N*K*N, B=N*K*U, C=U*K*U.
M is positive semidefinite and M >= B^*A^+B-C on U.
D realizes M on the finite trace image R(U).

finiteSchurDouglasAlgebraStatus.label: finite Schur/Douglas algebra
finiteSchurDouglasAlgebraStatus.reason: Completing the square with A^+ gives <n+u,K(n+u)>+<u,Mu> = <A^{1/2}n+A^{+1/2}Bu, A^{1/2}n+A^{+1/2}Bu> + <u,(C+M-B^*A^+B)u>.

finiteRangeConditionStatus.label: finite Douglas range condition
finiteRangeConditionStatus.reason: The nullspace component of B is exactly the obstruction; (I-AA^+)B=0 is equivalent to the cross term being in Range(A^{1/2}).

finiteTraceRealizationStatus.label: finite trace-side realization
finiteTraceRealizationStatus.reason: On the finite trace image, choosing D so that R_U^*DR_U=M transfers the U-block repair to the original finite form.

formalProof: Use the Moore-Penrose inverse A^+ on closure Range(A).
Apply the Douglas range condition to write B=A A^+ B.
Complete the square in the N variable.
Pull M back through the trace image realization D.
